item.title: Goldman’s Olson Sees Private Credit Market Growing Despite Fears
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s global head of alternatives for wealth expects private credit firms to keep drawing capital despite recent redemption episodes, due to the premium illiquid investments command.

item.title: BlackRock Strategist ‘Definitely’ Likes Korean Stocks on AI
item.description: BlackRock Inc. said South Korean equities are a key driver behind its latest upgrade of emerging-market stocks, citing sharp earnings growth and leverage to global semiconductor demand.

item.title: Dry-Bulk Shipping Rates Hit Four-Month Peak on Capesize Demand
item.description: A key gauge of bulk shipping rates climbed to the highest since early December, driven by a surge in demand and tightening vessel supply.

item.title: Goldman Favors North Asia’s Tech-Heavy Stock Markets Over South
item.description: North Asia’s tech-heavy equity markets offer better risk-reward than their South and Southeast Asian peers that are relatively more vulnerable to the oil shock triggered by the Iran war, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

item.title: Bonds Overprice India’s Inflation Risk, Top Fund Manager Says
item.description: For one of India’s biggest bond fund managers, markets are overpricing the inflation risk from the Iran war and that is creating a buying opportunity.

item.title: Iran's So Far Away, Stocks Can Hit a Record
item.description: Despite great uncertainty from the war, the S&P 500 never fell 10% from its peak.

item.title: Así cambian los pueblos cuando llegan miles de millones en centros de datos
item.description: Las grandes tecnológicas dicen que el despliegue de la inteligencia artificial en Aragón está teniendo tanto éxito que debería servir de modelo para otras regiones de la Unión Europea. Para los vecinos, la realidad es más compleja.

item.title: What Happens When $90 Billion of Data Centers Come to Town
item.description: Big Tech companies say northern Spain’s AI buildout is going so well, it should be a model for other parts of the EU. For local residents, the reality is more complicated.

item.title: Alibaba Moves Onto Tencent’s Turf With New AI Model for 3D Video
item.description: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. launched a new AI model that can be used to develop games and generate videos simulating the real world, expanding its suite of products to challenge Tencent Holdings Ltd. as it looks to monetize the technology.

item.title: Dollar’s Real Competition Is Decades Away, Franklin’s Desai Says
item.description: The dollar’s dominance has been the subject of much scrutiny but Franklin Templeton sees the greenback remaining the currency of choice.

item.title: Australia Ramps Up Defense Spending, Sees Parallels to WWII Era
item.description: Australia will raise defense spending to 3% of gross domestic product by 2033, Defense Minister Richard Marles said, including increased outlays on drones as it responds to rising conflict globally and China’s military buildup.

item.title: Japan’s Nikkei 225 Set for Record Close, Erasing Iran War Losses
item.description: Japan’s Nikkei 225 Stock Average is headed for a record high close that would erase its losses from the Iran war on optimism the new peace talks may hasten the end to the conflict.

item.title: US House to Weigh Penalties on AI Model Copying by Chinese Firms
item.description: House Republicans are calling for US sanctions against Chinese entities that improperly extract results from leading US artificial intelligence models to develop their own competing systems, part of a widening effort by Congress to counter China in the global AI race.

item.title: India Small Caps Return to Pre-War Levels as Risk Mood Improves
item.description: A gauge of smaller shares has jumped over 12% in April.

item.title: China Cuts Fuel Output and Lifts Aluminum After Gulf Shock
item.description: China processed less crude oil last month, as refiners cut run rates to conserve supplies snarled by war in the Persian Gulf.

item.title: Malaysia Plans Amending Laws to Curb Waste Dumping, Report Says
item.description: Malaysia is preparing to amend its environmental laws to give authorities greater power to curb illegal electronic waste as the country seeks to avoid becoming a global dumping ground for discarded components, according to a local media report.

item.title: China’s Economy Revs Up Despite War as Growth Tops Forecasts
item.description: China’s economic growth rebounded more than expected in the first quarter of 2026, suggesting limited spillovers so far from the war in Iran and giving policymakers more time to be patient with rolling out additional stimulus.

item.title: India’s Computer Science Grads Are Unprepared for the AI Revolution
item.description: Companies like Infosys are running their new hires through many weeks of training to bring them up to speed on new programming tools.

item.title: Musk Asks Suppliers to Move at ‘Light Speed’ on Terafab Plan
item.description: Elon Musk’s lieutenants have reached out to chip industry suppliers including Applied Materials Inc., Tokyo Electron Ltd. and Lam Research Corp. for his envisioned Terafab, early steps in an audacious and likely arduous attempt to break into the production of cutting-edge chips.

item.title: NJ Transit to Announce Pricey FIFA Ticket for World Cup Matches
item.description: New Jersey Transit plans to announce a special FIFA ticket on Friday that could cost more than $100 for riders going to MetLife Stadium for World Cup games.

item.title: Chilean President Kast Pitches Flagship Economic Bill Including Corporate Tax Cut
item.description: Chile President José Antonio Kast detailed legislation that’s the bedrock to his economic policies late on Wednesday, ahead of what will likely be lengthy debate and voting in the nation’s divided Congress.

item.title: Aerospace, Defense Parts Maker Arxis Raises $1.13 Billion in IPO
item.description: Arxis Inc., a maker of electronic and mechanical parts for aerospace and defense firms, raised $1.13 billion in an upsized initial public offering.

item.title: Australia Unemployment Holds at 4.3% Even as Full-Time Jobs Jump
item.description: Australian unemployment held steady in March as hiring persisted in the face of shockwaves from the US-Israeli war on Iran, which has driven up fuel prices and given another leg up to inflationary pressures.

item.title: China First-Quarter Growth Likely Rebounds Despite Iran War
item.description: An employee works on a carbon fiber production line at Zhongfu Shenying in Lianyungang, China's eastern Jiangsu province on July 31, 2025. (Photo by CN-STR / AFP via Getty Images) / China OUT

item.title: Taiwan Market Cap Tops $4 Trillion on AI Boom, Overtaking UK
item.description: Taiwan overtook the UK in stock market value as the island’s tech firms regained favor amid hopes for further de-escalation in the Iran war.

item.title: Indian Fund Outperforming 98% of Peers Bets on Defense Stocks
item.description: A $3 billion fund at Kotak Mahindra Asset Management Co. is backing Indian defense stocks, wagering that geopolitical tensions will boost local arms production and support the government’s efforts to lower reliance on imports.

item.title: Malaysia Plans to Use Tycoon Amin’s New Migrant Worker System
item.description: Malaysia is planning to adopt a new foreign worker recruitment system developed by Bestinet Sdn., the company founded by labor tycoon Aminul Islam, according to six people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Indian Officials See Iran War Oil Shock as Disruptive as Covid
item.description: Indian officials say the Iran war could be as disruptive to the economy as the Covid pandemic was six years ago and the damage could linger for years to come, threatening to knock the world’s fastest-growing major nation off its path.

item.title: Aussie-Yen Climbs to Highest Since 1990 as Risk Rally Builds
item.description: The Australian dollar rose to its strongest level in more than three decades against the yen, underscoring a rebound in risk sentiment amid optimism around a longer-term US-Iran ceasefire.

item.title: How Anthropic Learned Mythos Was Too Dangerous for the Wild
item.description: The AI company’s own experts warned Mythos could hack the systems beneath most modern computing. Banks and government agencies are racing to gauge the threat.
